# AMRE Real Estate Group
> Boutique luxury real estate advisory at Compass Beverly Hills, serving Los Angeles sellers, buyers, and investors.

AMRE Real Estate Group is a boutique team of licensed Realtors® and advisors led by Michael Abraham (licensed architect, Realtor®, CA DRE# 02242095) and Ania De Pourbaix (Realtor®, CA DRE# 01891438), operating under Compass Beverly Hills. The team specializes in luxury residential sales, multifamily investment, and buyer advisory across Los Angeles with 25+ years of combined experience and an architecture-trained perspective.

## Services

- Home selling representation (luxury, off-market, Compass Private Exclusive)
- Home buying representation and advisory
- Multifamily and duplex investment analysis
- 1031 exchange advisory
- Compass Concierge pre-sale home preparation
- Investment property underwriting and cap rate analysis

## Markets Served

Beverly Hills, Bel Air, Santa Monica, West Adams, Culver City, Mid-City, Malibu, Studio City, Brentwood, West Hollywood, Northridge

## Team

- Michael Abraham — Realtor®, Licensed Architect, CA DRE# 02242095, michael.abraham@compass.com, (323) 719-8585
- Ania De Pourbaix — Realtor®, CA DRE# 01891438, ania.d@compass.com, (310) 270-3879

## Featured Articles
- [Moving to Los Angeles: An Honest 2026 Relocation Guide](https://amre.group/blog/moving-to-los-angeles-2026/) — What it costs to move to LA (city median ~$1.0M, ~50% above national cost of living), best neighborhoods by lifestyle, rent vs buy, and a month-by-month relocation roadmap.
- [Moving to LA from New York City](https://amre.group/blog/moving-to-los-angeles-from-new-york-city/) — The honest NYC→LA comparison: Manhattan median ~$1.4M vs LA County ~$900K, 1-bed rent ~$4,680 vs ~$2,450, the tax myth (CA top rate is higher; LA has no city income tax), the hidden ~$800–1,100/mo car cost, and which LA neighborhoods fit New Yorkers.
- [Moving to LA from San Francisco](https://amre.group/blog/moving-to-los-angeles-from-san-francisco/) — A rare tax-neutral move (both California): median home ~$1.7M in SF (AI-boom highs near $2M) vs ~$900K in LA County, 1-bed rent ~$3,600 vs ~$2,550, and LA Silicon Beach (Culver City, Playa Vista, Santa Monica) as the tech landing pad.
- [Moving to LA from Chicago: What You Trade, What You Gain (2026)](https://amre.group/blog/moving-to-los-angeles-from-chicago/) — An honest Chicago→LA comparison: LA County median ~$900K vs ~$400K, 1-bed rent ~$2,550 vs ~$2,000, and IL flat 4.95% income tax vs CA up to 13.3% — you move for sun, coast, and a deeper job market, not to save.
- [Moving to LA from Seattle: Sun, Yes &mdash; But Mind the Taxes (2026)](https://amre.group/blog/moving-to-los-angeles-from-seattle/) — Seattle→LA: home prices comparable (~$870K vs ~$900K) and rent close, but the move goes from WA's 0% income tax to CA's up to 13.3% — you trade gray for sun and Silicon Beach, with a real tax increase.
- [Moving to LA from San Diego: The Intra-California Move (2026)](https://amre.group/blog/moving-to-los-angeles-from-san-diego/) — San Diego→LA is the gentle intra-California move: same taxes, LA median (~$900K) slightly below SD (~$1.0M), rents about equal — what changes is city scale and a broader job market.
- [Moving to LA from Washington, D.C.: Trade Policy for Palm Trees (2026)](https://amre.group/blog/moving-to-los-angeles-from-washington-dc/) — D.C.→LA: LA homes cost more (~$900K vs ~$695K) but income tax is comparable (D.C. up to 10.75% vs CA 13.3%) — you trade humidity and policy for a dry coast and LA's entertainment/tech/aerospace economy.
- Michael Abraham — Licensed Architect & Realtor® | AMRE at Compass Beverly Hills: https://amre.group/team/michael-abraham/ — Michael Abraham is one of the only people in Los Angeles who holds both a California architecture license and a California real estate license (DRE# 02242095). Founder of AMRE Real Estate Group at Compass Beverly Hills. Specialties: luxury and architectural homes, value-add multifamily, 1031-exchange strategy, hillside/canyon property. Markets: Beverly Hills, Bel Air, Brentwood, Santa Monica, Malibu, Studio City, Culver City, West Adams, Sherman Oaks, Playa del Rey. Phone: (323) 719-8585, email: michael.abraham@compass.com. The architect-agent angle: on a tour, evaluates load-bearing walls, renovation cost and scope, lot/zoning upside, structural integrity, and long-term hold value — not just list price.
- Ania De Pourbaix — Realtor® | AMRE at Compass Beverly Hills: https://amre.group/team/ania-de-pourbaix/ — Ania De Pourbaix is a licensed Realtor® and co-principal of AMRE Real Estate Group at Compass Beverly Hills, specializing in luxury and architectural real estate across Los Angeles's Westside. CA DRE# 01891438. Phone: (310) 270-3879, email: ania.d@compass.com. LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/ania-de-pourbaix/. Compass profile: compass.com/agents/ania-depourbaix/. Markets: Beverly Hills, Bel Air, Santa Monica, Culver City, West Hollywood, and wider LA metro.
- Beverly Hills Post Office Condos (BHPO) — A 2026 Buyer's Guide: https://amre.group/blog/beverly-hills-post-office-condos-guide/ — Explains what Beverly Hills Post Office (BHPO / Beverly Crest) actually is: a hillside neighborhood in the City of Los Angeles that carries a 90210 mailing address. Boundaries: Mulholland Drive to just above Sunset, Benedict Canyon to Coldwater Canyon. Key nuance: despite the 'Beverly Hills, CA 90210' mailing address, residents get LAUSD schools and LA city services, not Beverly Hills city services or BHUSD. Pricing: the broader 90210 area averages ~$6-7M sale price; lower BHPO can trade at or below $1,000/sqft. Condos and townhomes are rare in BHPO — typically 1-2 active at any time, appearing in smaller complexes near the lower edge. Four major gated communities: Beverly Park (ultra-luxury, multi-acre estates, $10M+), Mulholland Estates ($5M-$15M+, ridge views), The Summit ($4M-$12M+, panoramic LA basin views), Beverly Ridge Estates ($3M-$8M+, smaller/quieter). The 90210 address question: prestige is real but BHPO is not a substitute for Beverly Hills proper for school-district reasons. Architect-agent angle: hillside construction (retaining walls, soils, drainage, geotechnical) requires architectural literacy to assess correctly. Approximate figures, informational only.
- Condos for Sale in Venice, CA (2026 Buyer's Guide): https://amre.group/blog/condos-for-sale-venice-ca/ — The most attainable way into Venice, CA. As of mid-2026 the all-home median in Venice is ~$2.5M while condos commonly trade ~$900K–$1.6M for 1–2 bedroom units (area ~$1,150/sqft); condos are ~25% of for-sale inventory. Venice values softened modestly YoY; DOM ~high-50s. Where condos cluster: boutique low-rise and warehouse-conversion lofts near the Venice Canals and Abbot Kinney; larger Marina-adjacent buildings on the southern edge; smaller buildings on the ocean-adjacent walk streets. Two deal-deciders in 2026: HOA finances (budget, reserves, special assessments) and master insurance (California's tightening insurance market is pushing HOA premiums up or causing carrier exits, which flow to dues/assessments). 5-point condo diligence checklist (reserves, assessments, master insurance, CC&Rs/rental caps, litigation). Architect-agent angle: AMRE's licensed-architect + licensed-Realtor reads the building — conversion construction, envelope issues, sound, renovation feasibility within HOA rules. Approximate figures, informational only.
- Do You Need a Buyer's Agent in Los Angeles? (2026 Guide): https://amre.group/blog/do-you-need-a-buyers-agent-in-los-angeles/ — Answers "do you need a buyer's agent" for LA buyers as of June 2026. No legal requirement to use one, but a licensed agent must have a signed written buyer-broker agreement before touring any property (NAR settlement Aug 2024 + California AB 2992, effective Jan 2025; California caps each term at 90 days). Buyer-agent compensation is off the MLS and negotiated — the buyer is responsible under the agreement but can have the seller cover it as a concession (still common in LA). Covers what a buyer's agent actually does (find/vet incl. off-market access, value advice, negotiation, inspections/due diligence, escrow protection), a 5-point pre-signing checklist (compensation, 90-day term, exclusive vs non-exclusive, scope, cancellation), and the architect-agent edge: AMRE is led by a licensed architect + licensed Realtor who reads structure, renovation cost, and zoning/upside on every tour. Informational only, not legal advice.
- Cash & Flow: 4623 6th Ave Duplex Breakdown ($949K, Park Hills Heights): https://amre.group/cash-and-flow/ — AMRE's investment desk runs the numbers on a vacant, renovated two-unit Spanish duplex listed at $949,000 in Park Hills Heights, Los Angeles (90043). Two 2bd/1ba units, est. $2,600/mo each ($5,200/mo, $62,400/yr gross), ~15.2x GRM; 25% down ~$237,250, ~$711,750 loan, ~$4,735/mo at ~7%. House-hack angle: live in one unit, rent the other to offset most of the mortgage. Delivered fully vacant (set market rents day one, no inherited leases), LAR2 zoning on a 6,749 sf lot, near USC, Leimert Park, and the Crenshaw/LAX Metro. Illustrative estimates only; verify independently.
- Who Pays the Real Estate Agent? (2026 LA Buyer's Guide): https://amre.group/blog/who-pays-the-real-estate-agent-los-angeles/ — Answers the rising "do you pay a real estate agent if you are the buyer" (+3,700% search) and "who pays the real estate agent" queries. Since the NAR settlement took effect Aug 17, 2024, buyer-agent compensation is decoupled from the MLS and negotiated on every deal: the seller may cover it as a concession (still common in LA), the buyer may pay directly, or it's blended as a closing-cost credit. Commissions are always negotiable with no set rate (historically 5-6% total). A buyer working with an MLS-participant agent signs a written buyer-representation agreement before touring. LA stakes: a 2.5% buyer-agent fee on a $2M home is $50,000. Covers the three payment scenarios, what to check in the buyer-broker agreement, and how AMRE structures buyer offers so the commission is handled deliberately. Informational only; rules as of June 2026 and under appeal.
- The Equity You Built, the Rate You Locked (Sell or Redeploy?): https://amre.group/blog/home-equity-low-rate-sell-or-redeploy/ — For longtime LA owners holding a sub-3% mortgage and six figures of equity: a framework for whether to sell, borrow, or sit tight (as of June 2026). Purchasing-power compression — the same monthly payment that carried a $400K loan at 2.75% supports only ~$258K at today's ~6.5% rate, about 35% less. Redeploy math — $500K of equity redeployed across two ~4%-appreciating properties projects ~$209K more net equity at 5 years and ~$469K more at 10 years versus staying put (leverage: $200K down on an $800K asset = a 16% first-year gain on cash from 4% appreciation). Access routes: selling frees 100% of equity vs. an ~80-85% combined-loan-to-value cap when borrowing (HELOC ~7.2-7.5%, fixed home-equity loan ~7.4-7.9%, prime 6.75%); cash-out refinance usually unwise because it re-prices the whole sub-3% balance. California Prop 19 lets owners 55+/disabled/disaster-affected carry their low Prop 13 tax basis to a replacement home up to 3 times; primary-residence capital-gains exclusion is $250K single / $500K married.
- Luxury Real Estate Agent in LA (2026) — What Actually Makes One Different: https://amre.group/blog/luxury-real-estate-agent-los-angeles-2026/ — Comprehensive analysis of what distinguishes a real luxury LA Realtor from a high-priced generalist in 2026. Captures rising 'luxury real estate agent' search (+80% YoY). Five structural capabilities: (1) off-market and pre-MLS access via Compass Private Exclusives, broker-to-broker pocket listings, pre-MLS coming-soon inventory — ~15-20% of LA luxury transactions, 30%+ above $10M; the single biggest differentiating variable. (2) Specialized expertise matching inventory type (architectural/hillside/historic/contemporary new construction) — luxury is not a single market. (3) Communication and bespoke decision tooling sized to $5M+ decisions — custom dashboards, property microsites, design-press-quality presentation. (4) Negotiation track record at the relevant price tier (the buyer pool, cash dynamics, and contingency patterns differ above $3M). (5) Direct design-press editorial relationships (Architectural Digest, Dwell, Wallpaper, Curbed, LA Times Hot Property). The architect-Realtor angle: AMRE's Michael Abraham licensed-architect + licensed-Realtor dual credential (fewer than a dozen in LA) is structurally consequential for architectural and historic inventory. Outcome gap between top and average luxury agent at $5M: typically $200K+. Five interview questions framework. Cross-links to Architect Study, AI piece, and How to Choose piece.
- How to Choose a Real Estate Agent in LA (2026) — The Honest Rubric: https://amre.group/blog/how-to-choose-real-estate-agent-los-angeles-2026/ — Pillar guide for LA buyers and sellers evaluating real estate agents in 2026. Captures the rising 'best real estate agent' (+110% YoY) and 'real estate agent reviews' (+250%) search clusters. Seven-criteria rubric: (1) recent sub-market track record (12-24 months), (2) off-market and pre-MLS access via Compass Private Exclusives and broker network (~15-20% of LA luxury transactions), (3) specialized expertise matching buyer need, (4) communication style and bespoke decision tooling, (5) honest references from recent clients, (6) negotiation track record (list-to-sale ratio, DOM), (7) clear differentiation answer. Four red flags: generic luxury claims without specific sub-market transactions, all-time vs recent stats, no concrete off-market mechanics, AI valuation as primary pricing tool. Six interview questions framework. 'Near me' trap analysis — office location ≠ sub-market expertise. Buyer-side vs seller-side criteria weighting. LA-specific expertise definitions: sub-market judgment within sub-markets, architectural literacy, off-market network depth, climate/condition diligence. Cross-links to AI vs Realtor piece, Mid-Year 2026 update, and Architect-Designed Home Study.
- AI Real Estate Agent vs Human Realtor in 2026 (LA Buyer's Guide): https://amre.group/blog/ai-real-estate-agent-vs-human-realtor-los-angeles-2026/ — Comprehensive framework for LA home buyers weighing AI tools vs hiring a Realtor. Disambiguates 'AI real estate agent' as AI tools positioned as Realtor substitutes (not autonomous software agents). Five AI tool categories that work for LA buyers: (1) AI valuation tools (Zestimate ±5-10% standard, ±20-40% architectural), (2) AI search assistants (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity for concepts/disclosures), (3) listing platform AI (Zillow/Redfin/Compass filters), (4) AI mortgage calculators, (5) AI document review (inspection reports, HOAs, TDS/SPQ). Five things AI tools structurally cannot do: (1) off-market access (~15-20% of LA luxury via Private Exclusives/pocket listings), (2) negotiation and counterparty reading, (3) physical and architectural condition assessment, (4) block-level local judgment, (5) bespoke decision tools for specific buyer situations. Case study: anonymized $1.2M-budget buyer for whom AMRE built a custom buyer-orientation dashboard in 48 hours (6-step timeline, mortgage calculator with single-family/duplex toggle, 5 sub-market comparison) — AI accelerated the build; market judgment came from the agent. Hybrid framework: AI for research/calculation/document review; human for agent selection, negotiation, contingency strategy, inspection.
- Architect-Designed Homes in LA — Appreciation Study (30-Year Data): https://amre.group/blog/architect-designed-homes-appreciation-los-angeles/ — Comprehensive analysis of whether named-architect homes in Los Angeles (Schindler, Neutra, Lautner, A. Quincy Jones, Pierre Koenig, Ray Kappe, Buff & Hensman, Craig Ellwood) appreciate faster than the broader market. 30-year pattern study. Key finding: documented, scholarly-recognized, original-fabric examples have outperformed broader LA averages by 25-50% per square foot consistently. Three variables separate appreciation winners from losers: (1) provenance documentation (original LADBS permits, archival records at UCSB ADC/Getty/institutional collections, scholarly publication), (2) scholarly recognition (monograph publication, AIA awards, major surveys), (3) original-fabric integrity (period-appropriate windows/cabinetry/finishes, no unsympathetic modernization). Mills Act discussion (40-60% property tax reduction for qualified historic properties in LA HPOZs). Buyer's diligence framework. Authored by Michael Abraham, one of the few licensed architects who is also a licensed Realtor in LA.
- LA Real Estate Mid-Year 2026 — Halftime Report: https://amre.group/blog/los-angeles-real-estate-mid-year-2026-market-update/ — H1 2026 halftime state of the LA real estate market. LA County median: $895K-$942K (up 0.1-0.6% YoY). LA City median: ~$1.0M (+2.5% YoY). Mortgage rates: 6.0-6.5% 30-year fixed. 77% of CA homeowners have sub-5% mortgages (structural rate lock-in). Inventory loosening but below normal. DOM: 52-56 days. Sub-area divergence: Sunset Park 90405 +42.9% YoY (post-fire displacement + airport-park), Mid-City North +6%, BH luxury flat, upper-tier hillside -3%, WLA condos -5%. Three buyer watchpoints for H2 2026: rate direction, inventory normalization, insurance availability and cost.
- Westside vs Valley LA 2026 Decision Framework: https://amre.group/blog/westside-vs-valley-los-angeles-buyers-guide-2026/ — Comprehensive comparison of Westside vs San Fernando Valley for LA buyers. Climate: Valley daytime summer highs ~10°F warmer than Westside; extreme heat events can reach 20-22°F gap (Santa Monica low-70s vs Burbank 95+°F same day). AC essential in Valley, optional on most of Westside. Price: Valley 30-50% lower per sqft vs comparable Westside neighborhoods. At $1.5M: Westside buys 900-1,100sqft 1-2bed condo; Valley buys 1,800-2,200sqft 3-bed SFH with yard. Schools: SMMUSD and BHUSD strongest on Westside; Carpenter Community Charter (Studio City) top LAUSD elementary competitive with mid-tier SMMUSD. Commute: Valley wins for Burbank/NoHo studios (WB, Disney, ABC, NBC) — 10-15 min from Studio City; Westside wins for Culver City/Santa Monica studios. Walkability: Westside dominant (Walk Score 85-95 coastal). Decision framework: three questions — where you commute, absolute school quality need, and actual summer lifestyle.
- Living in Mid-City North LA (2026 Buyer's Guide): https://amre.group/blog/living-in-mid-city-north-los-angeles-neighborhood-guide/ — Dedicated guide to the northern half of Mid-City Los Angeles (ZIP 90019), the area between the 10 Freeway and Beverly Boulevard. Median home price ~$1.29M (mid-2026), median $/sqft ~$750. Sub-pockets: Faircrest Heights, Victoria Park (north), south of Pico, Mid-Wilshire spillover. Walking distance to LACMA, The Grove, Original Farmers Market; 3-min Metro Purple Line ride to Miracle Mile (Wilshire-La Brea station opened 2026). Schools: LAUSD, varies by block (Cienega, Cahuenga, Carthay Center ES → John Burroughs MS → LA Senior High). Prices ~30% above Mid-City South for equivalent housing.
- Living in Mid-City South LA (2026 Buyer's Guide): https://amre.group/blog/living-in-mid-city-south-los-angeles-neighborhood-guide/ — Dedicated guide to the southern half of Mid-City Los Angeles (ZIP 90016, locally "Mid-City Heights"), south of the 10 Freeway toward Washington Boulevard. Borders West Adams. Median home price ~$910K (30% below Mid-City North), median $/sqft ~$610. Sub-pockets: Lafayette Square (HPOZ, $1.5M-$2.5M+ restored), Wellington Square, Mid-City Heights core, West Adams-border streets. Concentration of restored 1920s-30s Spanish Colonial Revival, Craftsman bungalows, period revivals. Four Metro E (Expo) Line stations within reach: La Cienega/Jefferson, Farmdale, Expo/Crenshaw, Expo/Western — direct rail to USC, DTLA, Culver City, Santa Monica. Strong restoration market for architecturally literate buyers.
- Studio City Condos for Sale (2026 Buyer's Guide): https://amre.group/blog/studio-city-condos-for-sale/ — Comprehensive buyer's guide to Studio City condos as of mid-2026: median list price ~$742K, average price/sqft ~$620, ~43 days on market, 36-62 active listings. Inventory shape by price band ($500K-$750K is 32% of inventory, $750K-$1M sweet spot is 30%). HOA dues typical $400-$700/mo (older 1970s-80s), $700-$1,200/mo (newer mid-rise). Sub-areas: Moorpark corridor, Tujunga Village, South of Ventura (Carpenter Charter zone), North of Magnolia. Carpenter Community Charter elementary drives a measurable price premium in-boundary. Studio City vs Westside: same budget buys 30-40% more square footage in Studio City. 7-step HOA diligence checklist.
- https://amre.group/blog/named-architect-homes-los-angeles/ — A Buyer's Guide to Named-Architect Homes in Los Angeles: Schindler (15–35% premium, ~180 LA works), Neutra (20–45%, ~150 extant residences), Lautner (40–80%, ~45 works), Buff & Hensman (10–25%, post-and-beam, Altadena concentration). Attribution verification via USC, Neutra Institute, Getty. HPOZ + landmark constraints apply to most.
- https://amre.group/blog/mid-century-modern-los-angeles-buyers-guide/ — Mid-Century Modern in Los Angeles: What to Know Before You Buy: 650+ listed, $200K–$600K restoration range, 40+ HPOZ zones. Silver Lake / Los Feliz 38%, Hollywood Hills 28%, Altadena 18%. 6 key issues: flat roof, glazing, structural permits, HPOZ, mechanical, site drainage. Title 24 2026 energy code creates tension with historic preservation requirements.
- https://amre.group/blog/architect-eye-los-angeles-home-inspection/ — What the Architect's Eye Catches That the Inspection Misses: 7 things a licensed architect reads in LA homes that standard inspections miss — permit record gaps, structural load paths, HPOZ constraints, hillside drainage intent, material aging curves, and glazing system failure. Remediation cost ranges $8K–$120K when discovered post-close.

- 1031 Exchange in Los Angeles: 2026 Investor Primer: https://amre.group/blog/1031-exchange-los-angeles/ — Working primer on Section 1031 like-kind exchanges for LA real estate investors. Federal rules: 45-day identification window (Three-Property Rule most common), 180-day completion window (or tax filing deadline, whichever is earlier), qualified intermediary required, same-taxpayer rule. California-specific: clawback rule (FTB tracks deferred gains when CA property is exchanged out-of-state), annual Form 3840 filing requirement, mandatory 3.33% withholding at sale (Form 593 to claim exception). LA-specific: Measure ULA (4% above $5.32M, 5.5% above $10.64M) is NOT deferred by 1031 — owed at sale of relinquished property. Common scenarios: trade-up within LA (West Adams/Culver City/Mid-City duplex to larger multifamily), geographic diversification (LA to TX/AZ/NV), type change (active rental to passive DST). On a $1M LA gain, combined federal+CA tax can exceed 30% — 1031 defers it; held-to-death stepped-up basis eliminates it.
- How to Buy a Home in Los Angeles: 2026 Process Guide: https://amre.group/blog/how-to-buy-a-home-in-los-angeles/ — Step-by-step LA home buying walkthrough. Typical 30-45 day timeline (cash 7-14). Buyer closing costs 2-5% of price ($18K-$45K on a $900K home). California RPA default contingencies: inspection 17 days, appraisal 17 days, loan 21 days, with active removal (CR Form required). EMD 1-3% due to escrow within 3 business days of acceptance. SoCal custom: seller pays the owner's title insurance. LA-specific items: LA City transfer tax $4.50/$1K vs $1.10 county, Measure ULA mansion-tax thresholds ($5.32M / $10.64M), fire-zone disclosure, earthquake retrofit, rent-control compliance on duplex/triplex.
- Compass Private Exclusives, Explained: How LA Off-Market Works in 2026: https://amre.group/blog/compass-private-exclusives-explained/ — Plain-language explainer of Compass Private Exclusives (the off-market layer of LA real estate). ~5,500 active Private Exclusive listings nationwide as of 2026, distributed across 340,000 agents via Compass + Redfin (Feb 2026 deal) + MRED PLN (Apr 2026 nationwide). Three-phase strategy: Private Exclusive → Coming Soon → Public MLS. February 2026 Compass-Redfin agreement now displays Compass exclusives on Redfin; March 2026 Compass-Zillow lawsuit resolved with Zillow allowing pre-market exposure. In LA, the off-market layer carries an outsized share of trophy inventory in Beverly Hills, Bel Air, Brentwood, Carbon Beach, Point Dume, and architecturally significant homes.
- Tenancy in Common (TIC) in Los Angeles: 2026 buyer's guide (https://amre.group/blog/tenancy-in-common-tic-los-angeles-guide/) — TICs price ~10–20% below comparable condos; fractional loans ~15% down up to ~$2M; common in Silver Lake, Echo Park, West Hollywood; covers TIC vs condo, financing, risks, due diligence.
- Living in Playa del Rey: 2026 neighborhood & market guide (https://amre.group/blog/living-in-playa-del-rey-neighborhood-guide/) — LA’s last small beach town; median sale price ~$1.0M (~$720/sqft), ~11,300 residents, ~54 days on market; relative value vs Venice/Santa Monica/Manhattan Beach.
- Living in Sherman Oaks: 2026 neighborhood & market guide (https://amre.group/blog/living-in-sherman-oaks-neighborhood-guide/) — median single-family ~$1.3M (~$700/sqft), ~64,800 residents, balanced market (~73 days on market, ~98% of asking, <2.5 months inventory).
- Does Measure ULA Build More Housing? (Data Analysis): https://amre.group/blog/measure-ula-housing-production-analysis/ — Evidence-based analysis of LA's Measure ULA transfer tax (the "mansion tax"). ULA adds 4% on sales above ~$5.3M and 5.5% above ~$10.6M (rising to ~$5.4M/$10.9M after June 30 2026), on the full gross value, and applies to ALL property types — not just single-family homes. The UCLA Lewis Center / RAND "Taxing Tomorrow" study (April 2025) establishes a causal link: ULA is reducing multifamily production by ~1,910 units/yr (an 18% drop) and ~168+ affordable units/yr, because it taxes the sale of newly built apartments. Cumulative revenue passed $1B by Jan 2026 across ~1,435 deals, but annual collections (~$280–413M) run far below the $600M–$1.1B projected. A repeal measure (Local Taxpayer Protection Act) was certified for the Nov 3 2026 ballot.
- Rent vs. Own a $1M Home in Los Angeles (Corrected Model): https://amre.group/blog/rent-vs-own-los-angeles-1-million/ — Correct apples-to-apples: same $200k either into a $1M LA home (20% down, 6.5% fixed) or an index fund at 8%. Comparable 2BR/2BA rent $3,500/mo; all-in ownership $7,223/mo. Owner equity beats renter portfolio by $215k at 5 years, $347k at 7 years, $573k at 10 years. Leverage: 6% appreciation on $1M = 30% cash-on-cash on $200k down. House-hacking a $1.1M duplex (5% down, tenant pays $2,600) cuts effective cost to ~$7,200/mo.
- The LA Market Right Now (Prices, Rates & Where the Opportunity Is): https://amre.group/blog/los-angeles-market-now-2026/ — A current read on the Los Angeles housing market as of May 2026: prices have plateaued (metro median ~$910K), inventory has loosened (~4.6 months supply, highest in California), and 30-year mortgage rates sit in the mid-6% range. The market has shifted from frenzy to stability, favoring buyers and well-prepared sellers.
- The LA Luxury Market Right Now ($2M–$10M Prices, Cash Buyers & Strategy): https://amre.group/blog/los-angeles-luxury-market-now-2026/ — A current read on the Los Angeles luxury segment ($2M–$10M) as of May 2026: premium zip codes show 3–5% annual appreciation, cash buyers dominate (≈64% at $2M–$5M, ≈85% at $5M–$10M, reducing rate sensitivity), the ULA "mansion tax" applies above $5.4M (4%) and $10.9M (5.5%) for closings after June 30 2026, and days on market stratify by tier (<30 days under $3M to 75–120+ days above $6M). Off-market and Compass Private Exclusive listings drive much of the top-end activity.

- Studio City Condo Market Report (91604): https://amre.group/studio-city-condo-report/ — Weekly read on the Studio City condo & townhome market. June 2026: median list ~$825,000 (~$603/sqft), median new-listing ~$807,000, 25 active listings, 44% of listings with price cuts, ~61 avg days on market (median 35), median rent ~$2,820, Market Action Index 37 (slight seller's advantage, cooled from 41). Four price tiers from ~$644K (1,059 sqft) to ~$1.149M (1,973 sqft). AMRE's recent sale at 11023 Fruitland #401 (Citron Modern Living) closed at $1,137,500 after 60 days on market.
